raw_text: Red Hat Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 sssd: out-of-bounds read in the sssd This Moderate impact flaw in the System Security Services Daemon (SSSD) can lead to a local Denial of Service. A local attacker can exploit an out-of-bounds read in the PAM passkey responder by sending a crafted authentication request, causing the SSSD service to crash. This affects Red Hat systems where SSSD is configured and local user access is possible. A flaw was found in the System Security Services Daemon (SSSD). The pam_passkey_child_read_data() function within the PAM passkey responder fails to properly handle raw bytes received from a pipe. Because the data is treated as a NUL-terminated C string without explicit termination, it results in an out-of-bounds read when processed by functions like snprintf(). A local attacker could potentially trigger this vulnerability by initiating a crafted passkey authentication request, causing the SSSD PAM responder to crash, resulting in a local Denial of Service (DoS).
